mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is still below the inflation run-rate implied by CPIAUCSL, markets price a longer tightening path and higher terminal rates, pushing the 10-year Treasury yield up over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS remains at least 200 bps below year-over-year CPIAUCSL inflation
regime_hint: inflation overshoot with delayed policy response
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL decelerates for two consecutive monthly prints while the 10-year yield falls or stays flat in more than 60% of triggered windows, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A downturn in RSAFS signals weakening nominal final demand, which lowers expected growth and inflation compensation, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to decline over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If 20-day Treasury yield changes are positive in more than 60% of months with negative RSAFS momentum, the signal is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates slackening labor demand and weaker wage pressure, which reduces expected inflation and growth, pulling the 10-year Treasury yield down over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: If yield changes are positive or unchanged in a majority of instances after a 0.3pp 3-month UNRATE rise, the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: A broad drop in INDPRO together with weak PAYEMS signals an economy-wide supply and labor slowdown that lowers real rate expectations and inflation breakevens, driving the 10-year Treasury yield lower within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the combined INDPRO-PAYEMS downturn does not coincide with lower 10-year yields in at least 60% of historical episodes, the interaction is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL remains elevated while FEDFUNDS is still far below neutral, bond investors anticipate faster policy normalization and a higher term structure, which lifts the 10-year yield over the following month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation exceeds FEDFUNDS by at least 3 percentage points
failure_condition: If the 10-year yield falls after repeated CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS gaps above 3pp, or if the yield response is directionless in more than 40% of triggered cases, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in HOUST signals housing demand destruction and a weaker construction pipeline, which reduces near-term growth expectations and pushes the 10-year Treasury yield down over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If 60-day yield changes are not negative after HOUST declines in most cases, or if the effect disappears when HOUST is stable, the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in UNRATE and stagnation in PAYEMS marks a deteriorating labor market, which weakens wage growth expectations and lowers the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises while PAYEMS fails to increase over the prior month
failure_condition: If yield moves are positive after the joint UNRATE rise and PAYEMS stall in more than 40% of triggered episodes, the interaction is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, an upswing in INDPRO with firmer PAYEMS signals stronger capacity utilization and firmer inflation pass-through, which lifts the 10-year Treasury yield over the next month.
failure_condition: If 20-day Treasury yields do not rise when INDPRO and PAYEMS both accelerate in recovery regimes, or if the sign flips negative in most such episodes, the hypothesis is rejected.
